name: Barbell Row
resistance_profile: Heavy horizontal pull; loads mid-back hard but taxes the lower back.
cues: Hinge to a stable torso angle
execution_steps: Stand with feet hip-width, bar over mid-foot, and grip it just outside your knees.
Hinge at the hips to a stable torso angle near parallel, knees soft, spine neutral.
Let the bar hang at arm's length, shoulders set.
Drive the elbows back and row the bar to your lower ribs.
Lower under control to full arm extension without standing up or using momentum.
good_when: You want a heavy, high-load horizontal pull to build overall back mass and strength.
You can hold a stable hinge with a neutral spine and want simple progressive overload.
bad_when: Your lower back is fatigued or injured, or you can't maintain a stable hinge.
You want to train the back near failure without systemic or lower-back fatigue (use a chest-supported row).
